statement: Evaluate \(1.15 \div 15\) correct to \( 3 \) significant figures.
Find the L.C.M. of \(4x^2yz\) and \(6xy^3\).
If \(A=2\pi r^2+2\pi rh\), then \(h =\)
Find the remainder when \(x^3-x^2+1\) is divided by \(2x+1\).

Which of the following expressions has/have \(b-c\) as a factor?
I. \(ab-ac\)
II. \(a(b-c)-b+c\)
III. \(a(b-c)-b-c\)
Solve \(1 \lt -3x+4 \lt 10\).
If \(\dfrac{2}{x^2-1} \equiv \dfrac{a}{x+1}+\dfrac{b}{x-1}\), find \(a\) and \(b\).

In the figure, \((x, y)\) is any point in the shaded region (including the boundary). Which of the following is/are true?
I. \(x \le y\)
II. \(x+y \le 4\)
III. \(x \le 6\)
Solve \(\begin{cases}x^2+y^2=13 \\ x+y=1\end{cases}\).
If \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are the roots of the equation \(2x^2+4x-3=0\), find \(\dfrac{\alpha}{\beta}+\dfrac{\beta}{\alpha}\).
Find the \(n\)-th term of the A.P. \(4, 2, 0, -2, \dots\).
The sum to infinity of a G.P. is \(2\). If the first term is \(\dfrac{3}{2}\), find the common ratio.

Shop A offers a \(10\%\) discount on a book marked at \(\$P\). Shop B offers a \(15\%\) discount on the same book marked at \(\$Q\). If the selling price of the book is the same in both shops, express \(Q\) in terms of \(P\).
In the figure, area of \(\Delta ACD\) : area of \(\Delta BCD =\)
In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. \(AB\) and \(AC\) are tangents to the circle at \(B\) and \(C\) respectively. Area of the shaded region \(=\)
In the figure, the area of \(ABCD\) is

In the figure, \(A\) and \(B\) are two right solid cylinders with the same base radius \(1\). If the heights of \(A\) and \(B\) are \(1\) and \(2\) respectively, find the total surface area of \(A\) / the total surface area of \(B\).
If \(0^\circ \le \theta \le 360^\circ\), solve \(2\sin\theta=-\sqrt{3}\).
\(\dfrac{\dfrac{1}{\cos\theta}-\cos\theta}{\tan^2\theta} \equiv\)
The figure shows the graph of \(y=\dfrac{1}{2}\cos 2x\). The point \(P\) is
If \(0 \le x \le \pi\), solve \(2\sin x+3\cos x=0\) correct to \( 3 \) significant figures.

The figure shows a cube. Which of the following is/are equal to \(\angle AGE\)?
I. \(\angle AGF\)
II. \(\angle BDF\)
III. \(\angle DEG\)
In the figure, find \(x\) correct to \( 3 \) significant figures.
In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. Find \(x\).
In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. \(PA\) is the tangent to the circle at \(A\) and \(CB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} PA\). Find \(x\).

The figure shows a right circular cylinder with \(AC\) being a diameter of its upper face. \(AB\) and \(CD\) are two vertical lines on the curved surface. A curve is drawn on the surface of the cylinder from \(B\) to \(C\). Find its shortest possible length.
In the figure, \(ABCDE\) is a regular pentagon and \(ABF\) is an equilateral triangle. Find \(x\).
If \(a, b\) and \(c\) are all positive, which of the following may represent the graph of \(ax+by+c=0\)?
The equation of the circle centred at \((a, b)\) and tangential to the \(x\)-axis is
Find the equation of the straight line which passes through \((3, -1)\) and is perpendicular to \(2x-y+1=0\).

The bar chart below shows the number of electronic dictionaries sold in a shop last week:

Of those electronic dictionaries sold last week, what percentage were sold on Sunday?

Which of the following cannot be read directly from a cumulative frequency curve?
I. Mean
II. Median
III. Mode
There are 10 parcels. Two of them contain one pen each. If a man opens the parcels at random, what is the probability that he can find the two pens by opening two parcels only?
In a certain game, the probability that John will win is \(0.3\). If he plays the game 3 times, find the probability that he will win at least once.
Simplify \(\dfrac{1}{x-1}+\dfrac{1}{x+1}+\dfrac{3x-1}{1-x^2}\).

\(m\) and \(n\) are multiples of \( 3 \) and \( 4 \) respectively. Which of the following must be true?
I. \(mn\) is a multiple of \( 12 \).
II. The H.C.F. of \(m\) and \(n\) is even.
III. The L.C.M. of \(m\) and \(n\) is even.
Let \(x \gt y \gt 0\). If \(\log(x+y)=a\) and \(\log(x-y)=b\), then \(\log\sqrt{x^2-y^2} =\)
If \(\left(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{3}-\dfrac{1}{2}\right)x=1\), then \(x =\)
If \( 3 \) is a root of the equation \(x^2-x+c=0\), solve \(x^2-x+c \gt 0\).
The curve in the figure is the graph of \(y=-x^2+bx+c\). Find the area of the rectangle \(OPQR\).
If the common difference of the A.P. \(a_1, a_2, a_3, \dots\) is \(d\), then the common difference of the A.P. \(2a_1+3, 2a_2+3, 2a_3+3, \dots\) is
The length of a rectangle is decreased by \(20\%\). If the area remains unchanged, find the percentage increase of its width.

The following table shows the compositions of Tea A and Tea B which are mixtures of Chinese tea and Indian tea:

If \(4\text{ kg}\) of tea A and \(10\text{ kg}\) of tea B are mixed, find the ratio of Chinese tea and Indian tea in the mixture.
The figure shows a frustum of a right circular cone. The radii of the upper face and the base are \(1\text{ cm}\) and \(2\text{ cm}\) respectively. If the height is \(3\text{ cm}\), find the volume.
In the figure, if \(\dfrac{\text{Area of triangle } CDE}{\text{Area of triangle } BCE} = \dfrac{1}{2}\), find \(\dfrac{\text{Area of triangle } CDE}{\text{Area of trapezium } ABCD}\).
In the figure, find \(\theta\) correct to the nearest degree.

In the figure, the bearings of two ships \(A\) and \(B\) from a lighthouse \(L\) are \(020^\circ\) and \(080^\circ\) respectively. \(B\) is \(400\text{ m}\) and at a bearing of \(130^\circ\) from \(A\). Find the distance of \(B\) from \(L\).
The figure shows a right prism with a right-angled triangle as the cross-section. Find the angle between the line \(BF\) and the plane \(ABCD\) correct to the nearest degree.

In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. \(AP\), \(AB\) and \(BR\) are tangents to the circle at \(P\), \(Q\) and \(R\) respectively. Which of the following must be true?
I. \(AP+BR=AB\)
II. \(OQ\) bisects \(\angle AOB\)
III. \(\angle AOB = \dfrac{1}{2} \angle POR\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a trapezium with \(AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} DC\). \(AH\) bisects \(\angle BAD\) and \(DH\) bisects \(\angle ADC\). Which of the following must be true?
I. \(\angle AHD=90^\circ\)
II. \(\angle ADC=\angle BCD\)
III. \(\angle BAD+\angle BCD=180^\circ\)
In the figure, \(DE : EF =\)
\(A(-3, 2)\) and \(B(1, 3)\) are two points. \(C\) is a point on the \(AB\) produced such that \(AB : BC = 1 : 2\). Find the coordinates of \(C\).
\(C_1 : x^2+y^2=4\) and \(C_2 : x^2+y^2=9\) are two circles. A chord \(AB\) of \(C_2\) touches \(C_1\). Find the length of \(AB\).
